steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" selection list.
Click the "Tab" option in the "Select an option" dropdown within the Teams Capability popup in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dropdown at the top of the VS Code window to choose the project root directory.

Click the "Zip App Package" option in the UTILITY section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar within Visual Studio Code. The red crosshair precisely marks the menu item, indicating the automation targets the app packaging function for publishing.
Click the "manifest.json" file option in the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "dev" environment option in the "Select an environment" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension on Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's "App package successfully built at" exist
Press the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P in Visual Studio Code to open the Command Palette, enabling quick access to commands and actions within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.
Type 'notifications: clear' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box to initiate the "Clear All Notifications" command.
Press the Enter key to select "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" in the Visual Studio Code command palette.

Trigger the "Command Palette" in Visual Studio Code by pressing the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P, interacting with the application’s top command input interface. This action enables quick access to commands within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension workspace, as shown in the sidebar.
Type 'zip app package' into the Visual Studio Code command palette to trigger the Microsoft 365 Agents: Zip App Package command.
Press Enter to select "Microsoft 365 Agents: Zip App Package" from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Press the Enter key to select the "manifest.json" file in the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog within Visual Studio Code, in the context of Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit development.
Press the Enter key to select the "dev" environment in the "Select an environment" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Apply the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P in the Visual Studio Code interface to open the Command Palette, with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel active on the left.
Type text: 'notifications: show' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input at the top of the interface, triggering command suggestions.
Press the Enter key in the Visual Studio Code command palette to select the "Notifications: Show Notification" command.
@assertion there's "App package successfully built at" exist
